Name: dynamic_sparsity_memory_layout
Title: Dynamic Sparsity-Aware Memory Layout Optimization for Sparse Transformer Kernels
Short Hypothesis: Adapting memory layout to the sparsity patterns of input data in real-time can significantly improve hardware utilization and reduce latency in sparse transformer kernels.
Related Work: Existing work like Coruscant and LiquidGEMM focuses on static sparsity patterns and quantization. CUDA-LLM and CudaForge automate kernel generation but do not dynamically adapt memory layout based on sparsity. This proposal distinguishes itself by introducing a real-time, sparsity-aware memory layout optimization mechanism.
Abstract: Sparse transformer models are increasingly used to reduce computational costs, but their performance is often limited by inefficient memory access patterns. This paper proposes a dynamic sparsity-aware memory layout optimization technique that adapts to the sparsity patterns of input data in real-time. By leveraging hardware feedback from profilers like Nsight Compute, our approach dynamically reorganizes memory layout to minimize latency and maximize hardware utilization. We introduce a novel kernel design that integrates this optimization into existing sparse transformer frameworks. Experimental results on commodity GPUs demonstrate significant improvements in latency and throughput, with up to 2.5x speedup over state-of-the-art sparse transformer kernels.
Experiments: Implement a baseline sparse transformer kernel using existing techniques (e.g., Coruscant).
Develop a prototype kernel with dynamic sparsity-aware memory layout optimization.
Profile both kernels using Nsight Compute to measure latency, throughput, and hardware utilization.
Evaluate on multiple sparsity patterns (30%-70%) and input sequence lengths (256-4096).
Compare against state-of-the-art sparse transformer kernels (e.g., FlashAttention, Sparse Transformer).
Metrics: p50/p99 latency, tokens-per-second, arithmetic intensity, achieved bandwidth.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Complexity in integrating dynamic memory layout optimization with existing sparse transformer frameworks.
Potential overhead from real-time adaptation may outweigh benefits for certain sparsity patterns.
Limited generalization to different hardware architectures beyond the tested GPU.
